Makes VM live backups reliable: if a live snapshot can't be created, BombVault now automatically falls back to a graceful backup instead of failing. Thanks again to **manilx** for the VM backup reports.

## Changed
- **VM live backup auto-falls-back to graceful.** When a live (no-downtime) snapshot can't be created — for example a disk that can't be snapshotted, or a VM with no snapshottable disk — BombVault no longer just errors out. It automatically does a graceful backup instead (shut down → back up → restart), so a VM backup always completes. The whole thing is still recorded as a single run.

## Notes
- The fallback only happens when the snapshot is never created, so the VM is left untouched at that point — it is safe to shut it down for the graceful backup. A failure *after* the snapshot exists (during the actual backup or the overlay commit) still leaves the VM running and surfaces a clear message, exactly as before; it does not fall back.
- If your live backups keep falling back, the underlying disk likely can't be snapshotted. Grab `virsh dumpxml "<vm-name>"` from the Unraid terminal and open an issue / forum post so the disk can be classified correctly.

Image: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:1.10.0` (also `:1`, `:1.10`, `:latest`), multi-arch (amd64 + arm64).
